_inspectorSnowReport.Report:
Friday  March 13 | 5:49 a.m

Bonjour Tremblant,

After a crazy week, Friday is finally here—and the mountain is back on track thanks to our tireless crews. We woke up to 6 cm of fresh snow, and what better way to start the weekend than enjoying it with us? Temperatures are just right: cold and crisp, with minimal wind, and the sunshine might even make an appearance. Perfect conditions to get outside and enjoy the mountain.

Freestyle riders will also be happy to find our 4 Terrain Parks, offering a variety of features for all levels—whether you're just getting started, working on your tricks, or pushing your limits.

First Tracks 
  • Access to designated lifts, on both North and South side, as early as 7:45 a.m.
  • Ski on designated North Side slopes at 8 a.m.
Have fun out there!

Mountain safety tip of the week: Remove your backpack.
In chairlifts, to ensure you are seated properly, remove your backpack and place it on your lap or, if space allows, on the seat. This reduces the risk of accidents or of straps getting caught in the lift.

Snow fall is recorded daily at 5 a.m from mid-mountain at North Side (550 m / 1,800 ft). This data, based on 24 hours, provides a picture of on-mountain conditions. Actual accumulation may differ from the official report. Be aware of changing conditions. Lifts and trails exist in a mountain environment and may close due to weather, usage and/or special maintenance.
_inspectorSnowReport.SafetyReport: Happy Friday! 6 cm of fresh snow. 65 runs and 4 terrain parks are open. Please note that all glades are currently closed. Two sectors are open for alpine touring and snowshoeing: Beauchemin and Nansen Ascension. Have fun and ski safe! Mountain safety tip of the week: Remove your backpack. In chairlifts, to ensure you are seated properly, remove your backpack & place it on your lap or, if space allows, on the seat.
_inspectorSnowReport.SafetyReportFrench: Bon vendredi! 6 cm,  65 pistes et 4 parcs à neige ouverts. Tous les sous-bois sont actuellement fermés. Deux secteurs ouverts pour la rando alpine et la raquette : Beauchemin et Nansen Ascension. Amusez-vous bien et skiez prudemment! Conseil sécurité de la semaine : retirez votre sac à dos. Dans les remontées mécaniques, pour être bien assis, enlevez votre sac à dos et placez-le sur vos genoux ou, si l’espace le permet, sur le siège.
